Player Analytics: As a club operator, I want to see a map of where my members live, so I can assess catchment area and plan marketing spend.
Player Analytics: As a club operator, I want basic demographic data (age, gender) on my members, so I can understand my player base.
Player Analytics: As a club operator, I want to see LTV per player, so I can prioritise retention efforts on high-value members.
Player Analytics: As a club operator, I want to identify churners — members who haven't played in 60+ days — so I can target them with re-engagement campaigns.
Site Performance Analytics: As a club GM, I want to see new member sign-ups per site per month, so I can measure growth rate.
Site Performance Analytics: As a club GM, I want to see a rolling count of active players per site over the last 30 days, so I can track engagement trends.
Site Performance Analytics: As a club GM, I want to know which courts get booked first, so I can make pricing and layout decisions based on player preference.
Site Performance Analytics: As a club GM, I want to see total revenue broken down by site, so I can identify which locations are underperforming.

body: Stage 3 (AI Phase 1) is the first AI delivery — gap detection, dynamic pricing, dashboard insights. This is where the Terraform expansion gets significant: two new workers, three new scheduled jobs, plus operational jobs (partition management, archive, materialized view refresh) that are easy to forget but mandatory.
title: AI Phase 1 Infrastructure

body: Stage 2 (production readiness) is the "before any AI worker ships" pass — VPC connector, Cloud Armor, monitoring, the Anthropic and Vertex AI access. It is easy to skip and will bite hard the first time production traffic hits the platform.
title: Production Readiness Infrastructure sections 2.1 (done), 2.2, 2.5, 2.6

body: Stage 1 (MVP hardening) lands first because every item in it is a gap that exists today and would block production go-live regardless of AI work — Cloud Storage, read replica, pgvector, production environment scaffold, Terraform remote state. None of it is AI work and all of it derisks what comes next.
title: MVP Infrastructure Hardening

Add feature (new endpoint) to get list of players associated with a specific club

body: For launch, the most important addition is capturing stripe_payout_id on payments — it's a single column, populated via the payout.paid Stripe webhook, and it's the one missing link that clubs and your finance team will actually need. The period-close immutability problem is real but can be deferred — most early-stage SaaS platforms operate on trust and audit logs rather than enforced immutability until they have a compliance requirement that forces it.
